title: Heat exchanger with reinforcing means
abstract: A heat exchanger for vehicle arranged to allow an internal circulation of at least one fluid, comprising at least a heat exchanger bundle and at least a duct (10) at least partially outside the heat exchanger bundle, the duct (10) penetrating into the heat exchanger bundle by a hole arranged in a terminal plate (8) of the heat exchanger bundle, the duct (10) comprising at least a collar (42) lining the hole, characterized in that the heat exchanger comprises a reinforcing mean (20) in touch with the collar (42) of the duct (10) and with the terminal plate (8) of the heat exchanger bundle. Application in motor vehicles
claims: Claims 1. Heat exchanger (1) for vehicle arranged to allow an internal circulation of at least one fluid, comprising at least a heat exchanger bundle (2) and at least a duct (10) at least partially outside the heat exchanger bundle (2), the duct (10) penetrating into the heat exchanger bundle (2) by a hole (58) arranged in a terminal plate (8) of the heat exchanger bundle (2), the duct (10) comprising at least a collar (40, 42) lining the hole (58), characterized in that the heat exchanger (1) comprises a reinforcing mean (20) in touch with the collar (40, 42) of the duct (10) and with the terminal plate (8) of the heat exchanger bundle (2). 2. Heat exchanger (1) according to the previous claim, in which the reinforcing mean (20) is of an annular shape and extends all around the duct (10). 3. Heat exchanger (1) according to any of the previous claims, in which the reinforcing mean (20) comprises a lower surface (74) in touch with the terminal plate (8) of the heat exchanger bundle (2). 4. Heat exchanger (1) according to any of the previous claims, in which the reinforcing mean (20) comprises at least a first flat section (60) and a second flat section (62), the first flat section (60) being at least in touch with the collar (40, 42) the duct (10), the second flat section (62) being at least in contact of the terminal plate (8). 5. Heat exchanger (1) according to the previous claim, in which the first flat section (60) extends in a first plan (76), which is parallel to a second plan (78) in which extends the second flat section (62). 6. Heat exchanger (1) according to the claim 4, in which the first flat section (60) and the second flat section (62) extend in inclined plans. 7. Heat exchanger (1) according to any of claims 4 to 6, in which the second flat section (62) includes at least two radial extensions (30) separated by at least an interval (32). 8. Heat exchanger (1) according to the previous claim, in which the interval (32) form a passage between a chamber (54) delimited by the terminal plate (8), the collar (40, 42) and the reinforcing mean (20), and an outside environment the heat exchanger (1). 9. Heat exchanger (1) according to any of the claims 4 to 8, in which the first flat section (60) is interposed between the collar (40, 42) and a neck (50) which surrounds the hole (58) of the terminal plate (8). 10. Heat exchanger (1) according to any of the previous claims, in which the reinforcing mean (20) is formed by a flange (22) delimited by a upper surface (66) and a lower surface (68). 11. Heat exchanger (1) according to the previous claim, in which the reinforcing mean (20) includes a first lower surface (72) and the second lower surface (74). 12. Heat exchanger (1) according to the previous claim, in which the first lower surface (72) is in touch with a neck (50) of the terminal plate (8), the second lower surface (74) being in touch with a portion (52) of the terminal plate (8) surrounding at least partially the neck (50). 13. Heat exchanger (1) according to the claim 11 or 12, in which the first lower surface (72) is in touch with a neck (50) of the terminal plate (8), the first lower surface (72) being in touch with the collar (40, 42) of the duct (10). 14. Heat exchanger (1) according to any of the previous claim, in which the reinforcing mean (20) is connected in common to the duct (10) and to the heat exchanger (1) by a metal contribution. 15. Heat exchanger (1) according to any of the previous claim, having a first internal path adapted to a refrigerant fluid and a second internal path adapted to a liquid coolant.
